idea.hook: Light travels at 670 MILLION mph. So why does space still feel impossibly far?
facts: Light reaches the Moon in just 1.3 seconds.
It takes light 8 minutes to travel from the Sun to Earth.
To reach the nearest star, Proxima Centauri, light needs over 4 years.
To cross our entire Milky Way galaxy, light takes about 100,000 years.
idea.payoff: The universe is so unimaginably vast, even light seems slow.
idea.title: Light Speed: Not As Fast As You Think! 🤯🌌

scenes.visual: Dynamic light streak racing across a dark, star-filled space background.
Earth and Moon, a light beam quickly flashes between them, then holds.
Sun and Earth in their orbits, a light beam travels from the Sun to Earth, visually demonstrating the time.
Zoom out from our solar system, revealing a vast field of distant stars, one star (Proxima Centauri) glows faintly in the distance.

scenes.text:
Milky Way: 100,000 YEARS.
Even light seems SLOW.
scenes.visual: Majestic, slowly rotating spiral galaxy (Milky Way), filled with countless stars and glowing nebulae.
